core_mechanism: A critical-dimension threshold on the minimal invariant (energy) cost of self-localized, time-periodic nonlinear excitations, set by how that excitation's energy scales as its frequency approaches the edge of the linear spectrum: the lower bound vanishes below a critical spatial dimension d_c and is strictly positive at or above it
brief_reason: The abstract asserts that the energy of these localized periodic lattice excitations has a positive lower bound only when the dimension is at least a critical value d_c, while below it arbitrarily low energies are possible, which is a dimension-dependent scaling threshold on the minimal cost of creating a localized mode near the linear band edge.
